title.en: Rename the Native Token from Toncoin (TON) to Gram (GRAM)

description.en:
## 🧾 Summary

Telegram proposes renaming the native token of The Open Network from Toncoin to Gram, and changing its ticker from TON to GRAM.

This is a name-and-ticker change only. The blockchain is not renamed – it remains TON / The Open Network. There is no swap, migration, bridge, claim, or conversion. Every balance, address, contract, and position stays exactly as it is today. A balance of 10 TON simply becomes 10 GRAM.

## 💡 Motivation

As part of the broader MTONGA (Make TON Great Again) initiative, we propose renaming the native token from Toncoin to Gram. The rename reflects a real shift in what TON has become, on two fronts that reinforce each other.

The first is structural. Telegram is becoming the driving force behind TON and the network's largest validator. This change in stewardship is what gives the technical roadmap its new pace and direction.

The second is at the protocol level. Recent upgrades have made the network 10 times faster, with subsecond transactions, and cut fees by roughly 6 times to nearly zero, alongside a strong focus on technical excellence.

Taken together, these changes mark a milestone for the network — and a natural moment to refresh how the token is known publicly. In the original TON White Paper, the network's founders called the native token Gram. The name has lived on inside the core TON codebase ever since. Surfacing it now is how the network shows it is stepping into a new chapter.

The change is not a technical update to the blockchain:

  • The blockchain keeps the name TON / The Open Network.
  • Only the native token's name (Toncoin → Gram) and ticker (TON → GRAM) change.
## ⚙️ Specification – what changes, what doesn't

Native token name: Toncoin → Gram
Ticker: TON → GRAM
Blockchain / network name: Unchanged — remains TON / The Open Network
Role as native asset: Unchanged
Balances: Unchanged — e.g. 10 TON becomes 10 GRAM
Gas fees: Still paid in the native unit, now displayed as GRAM
Addresses: Unchanged
Smart contracts: Unchanged — continue to work as before
Validators / staking: Unchanged — positions displayed as GRAM
NFTs & jettons: Unchanged
DeFi positions: Unchanged (lending, liquidity, staking, borrowing, etc.)
CEX balances: Unchanged — exchanges update name/ticker on their own schedule
Swap / bridge / claim / migration: None — no action required from users

## 🗓️ Timeline

The full process is designed to complete within 3 weeks from the start of voting.

1. Voting

Duration: Week 1 (7 days)
The community votes on TON Vote.

2. Rollout

Duration: Weeks 2–3 (14 days)

If the vote passes (more than 50% votes "For"), all platforms are expected to complete the transition within two weeks of the voting close, so that within three weeks of the vote opening, the name and ticker are updated consistently across the ecosystem — wallets, explorers, exchanges, and partners.

## 🗳️ Voting Process

We propose the following sequence of actions and timelines:

  • The vote runs for one week. Voting power is weighted by the TON balances at the end of the voting process.
  • The vote runs on the smart contracts of one of the largest open DAO platforms on TON — TON Vote.

Options: For / Against / Abstain

## ❓ FAQ

What is happening?
The native token Toncoin is being renamed to Gram, and the ticker is changing from TON to GRAM.

Is the TON blockchain being renamed?
No. The blockchain remains TON / The Open Network.

Is Gram a new token?
No. Gram is simply the new name and ticker for the existing native token previously known as Toncoin.

Do I need to swap my TON?
No. There is no swap, migration, bridge, claim, or conversion of any kind.

What happens to my balance?
It stays the same. 10 TON before the rename is 10 GRAM after the rename.

Are smart contracts affected?
No. Existing contracts continue to work exactly as before.

Are staking positions affected?
No. They remain unchanged and will simply be displayed as GRAM.

Are NFTs affected?
No. NFTs are unaffected.

Are DeFi positions affected?
No. Lending, liquidity, staking, borrowing, and other positions are unaffected.

Are exchange (CEX) balances affected?
No. Exchanges will update the display name and ticker on their own rollout schedule. Your balance does not change.

What happens to gas fees?
Gas continues to be paid in the native token of The Open Network – now displayed as GRAM.

What is TON after the rename?
TON remains the name of the blockchain and ecosystem. GRAM is the native token of TON.

How do I avoid scams?
Do not use any website, bot, or link that asks you to claim, swap, migrate, or convert TON to GRAM. No action is required from you. This is a display-name change only.

## ⚠️ Security Note

This is a name change only. No one needs to move, swap, claim, bridge, or convert anything. Any site, bot, or message instructing you to "migrate TON to GRAM" or "claim your GRAM" is a scam. Your funds are safe and require no action.
